Question Name: Is the organization subscribed to, or monitoring, appropriate sources for vulnerability and patch alerts for the components that it is responsible for?

Question Name: Is the organization responsible for any patching or maintenance of the system on the cloud service?
Question Description: If yes, additional questions will apply about the patch management process.

Question Name: Are you using your own equipment?
Question Description: Provide the details of who is providing the laptop, desktop, table, mobile device or other computer equipment that is used for access to the organization systems.

Question Name: Do you use a VPN to access the organization?
Question Description: Check 'Yes' if you use a Virtual Private Network to log into the organization network(s).

Question Name: Do you use 2 factor authentication when accessing the organization?
Question Description: Check 'Yes' if your company accesses the organization networks using multiple authentication factors (at least 2) to prove one's identity.

Question Name: Is the vendor using role based access when accessing the organization?
Question Description: Check 'Yes' if your company has limited access based on roles to the organization's systems.

Question Name: For any data that the vendor manages, processes, or stores: is it done in accordance with the relevant data protection and other regulations, with specific requirements formally established by the organization?
Question Description: Check 'Yes' if your company has a compliance program that defines how customer data is managed, processed and stored in relationship to privacy regulations and best practices.

Question Name: Has the vendor personnel read and understood the organization's email, password and encryption policies?
Question Description: Determine which polices are reviewed by the vendor staff and answer in accordance to the number of policies that the are reviewed and understood by the vendor's team working with the organization.

Question Name: Does anyone on the vendor team have admin access?
Question Description: Check 'Yes' if anyone associated with your firm has admin access to any the organization system(s).

Question Name: Who has admin access to the organization's digital assets?
Question Description: Write the names of the individuals with admin access to this asset and their emails.

Question Description: Describe why those individuals in your firm have admin access.

Question Name: Does your company require you to perform annual security awareness training?
Question Description: Check 'Yes' if you require employees and contractor to have annual security awareness training.

Question Name: Who is responsible to patch the system?
Question Description: If you are responsible to patch this system select 'Vendor'.  If you are not responsible to patch this system select 'Organization'.

Question Name: Attach proof of external penetration testing and remediation within the past 12 months.
Question Description: Attach a copy of your latest pen test.  Check  'N/A' if not applicable.

Question Name: How often do you collect and review server logs for application access?
Question Description: Select daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, bi-annually or never.

Question Name: How often do you collect and review logs for possible intrusion detection?
Question Description: Select daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, bi-annually or never.

Question Name: If data must be encrypted, are there provisions to ensure that the client's information is protected are using the proper ciphers?
Question Description: Indicate all ciphers used. The Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) is the trusted standard algorithm used by the United States government, as well as other organizations. Although extremely efficient in the 128-bit form, AES also uses 192- and 256-bit keys for very demanding encryption purposes. AES is widely considered invulnerable to all attacks except for brute force. Regardless, many internet security experts believe AES will eventually be regarded as the go-to standard for encrypting data in the private sector.

Question Name: Does your company require you to preform annual security awareness training of cloud vendor?
Question Description: Check 'Yes' if you require employees and contractors to have annual security awareness training.

Question Name: Are you using the organization's equipment?
Question Description: Check 'Yes' if your company obtains a laptop, desktop, tablet or mobile phone for your employees or contractors to use that is owned by the organization.

Question Name: Does the organization have patching requirements been documented and agreed upon in writing?
Question Description: Select 'Yes' if they are in writing and 'No' if they are not.

Question Name: Are the encryption keys kept on a separate server or storage block?

Answer: No - The key backup is not audited.
Question Description: Back up keys are essential to the protection of the data.

Answer: No - The key backup is audited every year.

Answer: Yes - They are refreshed twice a year.

Question Name: Who maintains the system?
Question Description: Vendors have more risk than your employees.

Question Name: What level of access does the vendor have to this system?
Question Description: Admin rights have the highest risk.

Question Name: Is the system using cloud specific technology - which type?
Question Description: SaaS has the highest risk and IaaS has the lowest risk.

Question Name: Is the cloud private, public or hybrid?
Question Description: Private clouds have the least risk and public clouds have the most risk.

Question Name: If the service provider is NOT responsible for patching all components that make up the cloud service, has the organization identified which components the service provider is responsible for and which it is responsible for?
Question Description: If the firm is not clear on the CSP responsibilities it has a very high level of risk.

Question Name: Has the organization ensured that all of the components that it is responsible for have been incorporated into its patch and vulnerability management process?  Is the process automated?
Question Description: Prioritization by financial exposures lowers risk more effectively.

Question Name: Does the service provider's incident response and management procedures map to (or fit with) the organization's internal policy and procedures; that does not hinder or delay the organization's ability to manage incidents in a timely and effective manner?
Question Description: The cloud service provider's incident response procedures must not delay the organization's ability to respond, otherwise it increases risk.

Question Name: What type of cloud specific technology is being used?
Question Description: Select Software as a Service (SaaS), Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), or Platform as a Service (PaaS).

Question Name: What is your Cloud Service Deployment Model?
Question Description: Select Private, Hybrid or Public.

Question Name: Is there a backup of all your keys  kept in an offsite location?
Answer: Yes - Key backups are kept offsite.
Question Description: Keeping keys offsite is a best practice.

Answer: No - Key backups are kept onsite.

Question Name: Are background checks done on employees and contractors?
Question Description: Provide the details of how your company performs background checks on all employees and contractors.

Answer: Yes - Criminal, Credit and Drug Tests
